chapter_title.en: Chapter on the Dislike of Traveling Alone

text: 2432 - رَوَى عَلِيُّ بْنُ أَسْبَاطٍ عَنْ عَبْدِ اَلْمَلِكِ بْنِ مَسْلَمَةَ عَنِ اَلسَّرِيِّ بْنِ خَالِدٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ : «أَ لاَ أُنَبِّئُكُمْ بِشَرِّ اَلنَّاسِ » قَالُوا بَلَى يَا رَسُولَ اَللَّهِ قَالَ «مَنْ سَافَرَ وَحْدَهُ وَ مَنَعَ رِفْدَهُ وَ ضَرَبَ عَبْدَهُ».

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.2432 - Ali ibn Asbat narrated from Abdul-Malik ibn Maslamah, from al-Sarri ibn Khalid, from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
The Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace and blessings of Allah (swt) be upon him and his family) said:
"Shall I inform you of the worst of people?"
They said: "Yes, O Messenger of Allah (swt)."
He (sw) said: "The one who travels alone, denies giving aid, and strikes their servant."
